items.myth: Data augmentation is the same as collecting more real data.
items.reality: Augmentation perturbs existing rows — it increases diversity along transform axes but cannot introduce wholly new scenarios, rare classes, or distribution shift your transforms never simulate.
items.example: Flipping cat photos does not teach the model about new breeds absent from the original set.

items.reality: Extreme jitter, rotation, or paraphrase can destroy label signal or make training unnecessarily hard — validation error can rise. Tune strength to the task.
items.example: 90° rotation on MNIST digits merges 6 and 9 classes — accuracy collapses.

items.reality: Text (paraphrase, synonym swap, back-translation) and tabular data (Gaussian noise, SMOTE for imbalance) also use augmentation-style regularization — with different validation risks.
items.example: SMOTE on credit fraud features creates synthetic fraud rows without new real transactions.

items.myth: If you augment, you do not need early stopping or L2.
items.reality: Augmentation addresses input diversity; early stopping and weight penalties still limit parameter memorization — production pipelines combine them.
items.example: CNN with strong augmentation still benefits from stopping when val loss rises after epoch 12.
